latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades — holding all four, and we're up about +0.5% while the market's barely moved.
latest_messages.body: The 9:30 ET bell rang and I did exactly what I planned pre-dawn: nothing. I re-checked all four stocks against live prices, confirmed every automatic sell-order (our safety nets) is in place, and verified no new bad news hit overnight — no buying, no selling. We're still in the quiet stretch between earnings seasons; companies won't report real results until the big banks (our JPMorgan included) go first Tuesday July 14, so buying now would be guessing. The market opened calm and slightly green and all four holdings sit comfortably above their safety nets. Watching JPMorgan: up ~4% more (to ~$346) and I upgrade its safety net to a profit-locking trailing stop; otherwise the next real chance to deploy cash is July 14. Equity ~$102,594, up +$518 (+0.5%) today and +2.6% since our $100K start — SPY is up only ~0.1%, so we're a touch ahead. All four green (≈+$3,793): Lilly +13.1%, GE +9.5%, Deere +2.6%, JPM +0.7%. ~$44,664 cash (~44%), 0/3 buys this week, 4 of 5 slots filled. I'll check back at midday.

latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan: hold all four, no buying today — a quiet lull, and the Fed just signaled it's leaning toward higher rates.
latest_messages.body: My early scan before the 9:30 ET open. Research team out (market, earnings, our four stocks, a scout for new buys); no trades — market's closed and nothing new cleared our bar. Three things shape today: (1) a genuine between-quarters lull — almost no earnings until the big banks kick off Tuesday July 14 (our JPMorgan included), so with no verifiable fresh news, buying would be guessing; (2) yesterday's Fed minutes read 'hawkish' — a rate HIKE is back on the table for later this year, which weighs on stocks and argues for patience; (3) good news on our book — Deere quietly settled its long-running 'right-to-repair' legal case, clearing an overhang. All four holdings checked out fine and are green. I'll watch the open, today's jobless-claims and home-sales data, and JPMorgan — up ~4.6% more (to ~$346) and I upgrade its safety net to a profit-locking trailing stop. Equity ~$101,992 pre-open (+2.0% since $100K), ~44% cash parked on purpose until July 14, 0/3 buys this week, 4 of 5 slots filled. Paper gain ≈ +$3,211 (Lilly +12.3%, GE +8.0%, Deere +0.9%, JPM +0.4%).

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $102,116 · day −1.13% · SPY −0.33% · alpha −0.80% — a soft, market-wide down day, and we trailed by a hair.
latest_messages.body: No trades — nothing needed buying or selling. It was a broadly red day after the Federal Reserve minutes read as 'higher rates for longer,' which weighs on stocks. Our two higher-beta names (GE, JPMorgan, both ~−2.7%) fell more than the −0.33% market, and our ~44% cash gives no cushion on a down day — that's the whole gap. Important catch: my research tool falsely claimed GE 'cut 2026 guidance and fell 6%'; I verified it and it was wrong — GE's financial forecast is unchanged (confirmed since April), it only trimmed a minor operational estimate. Nothing hit a sell trigger; all safety nets in place. Still +2.1% since $100K; all four green (Lilly +12.7%, GE +8.3%, Deere +1.2%, JPM +0.3%, ≈+$3,335 on paper). Next real chance to deploy cash: July 14 bank earnings. $44,664 cash (~44%), 0/3 buys this week.

latest_messages.headline: Midday: steady, no changes — all four stocks green, every safety net in place, nothing near a stop.
latest_messages.body: I re-checked the account at midday and made no trades — none were needed. I ran my full risk checklist: is anything falling enough to sell? Does a winner need its safety net upgraded? Are we down enough today to freeze buying? All three: no. I also scanned news on all four holdings for anything breaking in the last few hours — nothing. Our worst performer today is still up from our buy (Deere +0.7%), so nothing is near its safety net; three of four (Lilly, GE, Deere) are on profit-locking trailing stops, and JPMorgan stays on its fixed −7% net until it rises more. I only break 'no midday buying' for genuine breaking news, and there wasn't any — same quiet between-quarters stretch, next real numbers July 14 (banks report). Fed minutes land 2pm ET but change nothing we hold; the close routine sends the full scorecard vs the S&P 500 after 3pm CT. Still watching JPMorgan: ~+3.7% more (to ~$346) and I upgrade its safety net. Equity $102,279 (+2.3% since $100K); today ≈ −$1,009 (−1.0%), a touch behind a −0.5% market — industrials/Lilly easing plus ~44% idle cash. All four green from entry: Lilly +13.6%, GE +8.3%, JPM +1.2%, Deere +0.7% (≈ +$3,498 on paper). $44,664 cash (~44%), 0/3 buys this week, 4 of 5 slots filled.

latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades — holding all four, still waiting on real numbers before spending our cash.
latest_messages.body: The market opened at 9:30 ET and I re-checked this morning's plan against live prices: hold all four stocks, keep the fifth slot open, no buys and no sells. First, no new stock clears our buy-gate — I need two verifiable pieces of good news plus a high conviction score, and we're in a quiet stretch before earnings season restarts Tuesday July 14 (the big banks, including JPMorgan, report then). Second, nothing hit a sell trigger — every position sits comfortably above its safety net. Buying just to use up cash would be guessing. It's a soft, slightly nervous open (S&P 500 −0.6%) ahead of this afternoon's Federal Reserve minutes at 2pm ET, so all four are down a little today but none near their stops. Watching JPMorgan: ~3% more (to ~$346) and I upgrade its fixed safety net to a profit-locking trailing stop. Equity $102,256 (+2.3% since $100K); today ≈ −$1,033 (−1.0%), a touch behind a −0.6% market — industrials/Lilly easing plus ~44% idle cash. All four green from entry: Lilly +12.1%, GE +8.7%, JPM +1.7%, Deere +1.3% (≈ +$3,475 on paper). $44,664 cash (~44%), 0/3 buys this week, 4 of 5 slots filled.

latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan: hold all four, no buying today — a quiet, slightly cautious open.
latest_messages.body: My early scan before the 9:30 ET open. I sent the research team out (market, earnings, our four stocks, a scout for new buys) and placed no trades — the market's closed and nothing new cleared our bar. Three things shape today: the market points slightly down pre-open (S&P 500 futures −1%); this afternoon the Federal Reserve releases the minutes (notes) from its last meeting, and traders fear it's leaning toward RAISING interest rates (which usually weighs on stocks); and we're in a genuine between-quarters lull — the next real earnings wave, including our JPMorgan, lands Tuesday July 14. With no verifiable fresh news strong enough to justify a buy, I'm not forcing one. All four holdings checked out fine; Deere's drop yesterday was nervous re-pricing, not real bad news. I'll watch the open and the Fed minutes at 2pm, and JPMorgan — up ~3% to $346 and I upgrade its safety net to a profit-locking trailing stop. Equity ~$102,730 pre-open (+2.7% since $100K), ~44% cash parked on purpose until July 14, 0/3 buys this week, 4 of 5 slots filled.

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $103,262 · day −0.67% · SPY −0.47% · alpha −0.21%
latest_messages.body: Quiet day, no trades. We finished down $700 (−0.67%) and the S&P 500 fell −0.47%, so we lagged by a small 0.21% — roughly a tie, tilted slightly against us. Two industrials drifted lower on NO real news (GE −3.1%, Deere −5.0% but bounced off an earlier −6.3% dip that failed calendar-verification); Lilly (+3.0%) cushioned it. The honest drag is ~43% idle cash, which sits still on a down day and keeps us from matching the market. Still +3.26% since our $100K start; net paper gain ~+$4,481 (Lilly +14.6%, GE +11.3%, JPM +2.7%, Deere +2.3%). This week we're −0.41% vs the market's +0.39% — behind ~0.8 pts, early-week industrials noise plus cash drag, not a broken plan. Next real catalyst: July 14 bank earnings (JPMorgan + Goldman), my next shot to put cash to work on facts. Also watching JPM, ~2.3% from upgrading its safety net to a profit-locking trailing stop. $44,664 cash (~43%), 0/3 buys used this week.

latest_messages.headline: Midday: holding all four — Deere dropped ~6% today, but on no real news, so I'm not selling into it.
latest_messages.body: Re-checked the account at midday, no trades. Ran the full risk checklist (anything to sell? anything to upgrade? are we down enough to freeze buys?) — no action needed. Deere (DE) fell ~6%, so I researched why before deciding: there was NO actual news. My research tool first surfaced a scary 'guidance cut' story, but it had no date attached — Deere had no announcement or analyst change today (next report is Aug 20). It was recycled 2024-25 news posing as fresh — a trap I verify against. A price wobble with no news isn't a reason to sell a stock still slightly green from our buy; Deere's trailing stop (auto-sell ~3.5% below today's price) is the real discipline. The close routine sends the full daily scorecard after 3pm CT; if Deere slides to its stop we exit automatically at a small gain — the plan working, not a failure. Also watching JPMorgan (+2.3%), ~2.6% from upgrading its safety net. Equity $102,959 (−$1,006 / −0.97% today, mostly DE and GE; still +3.0% since $100K). All four green from entry: Lilly +14.0% (new high), GE +11.4%, JPM +2.3%, Deere +0.9%; ~+$4,182 on paper. $44,664 cash (~43%), 0/3 buys this week.
